contentBlocks.original:
सत्यं बृहदृतमुग्रं दीक्षा तपो ब्रह्म यज्ञः पृथिवीं धारयन्ति ।
सा नो भूतस्य भव्यस्य पत्न्युरुं लोकं पृथिवी नः कृणोतु ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
satyaṃ bṛhadṛtamugraṃ dīkṣā tapo brahma yajñaḥ pṛthivīṃ dhārayanti .
sā no bhūtasya bhavyasya patnyuruṃ lokaṃ pṛthivī naḥ kṛṇotu ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! the truth (satyam), the cosmic divine law (ritam), the spiritual passion manifested in mighty initiations, penances and self dedications to the search of brahman (by the sages); these have sustained the mother earth for ages (who in turn have supported these in her bosom), she, who is to us the consort of the past and the future (being its witness), may she expand our inner life in this world towards the cosmic life (through her purity and vastness).
contentBlocks.meanings: सत्यं (satyaṃ) – truth; बृहदृतमुग्रं (bṛhadṛtamugraṃ) – great, fierce cosmic law (bṛhat:great | ṛtam:cosmic law | ugram:fierce); दीक्षा (dīkṣā) – initiation; तपः (tapaḥ) – penance; ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman; यज्ञः (yajñaḥ) – sacrifice; पृथिवीं (pṛthivīm) – earth; धारयन्ति (dhārayanti) – sustain; सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – our; भूतस्य (bhūtasya) – of the past; भव्यस्य (bhavyasya) – of the future; पत्नी (patnī) – consort; उरुं (uruṃ) – vast; लोकं (lokaṃ) – world; पृथिवी (pṛthivī) – earth; नः (naḥ) – for us; कृणोतु (kṛṇotu) – may expand;

contentBlocks.original:
असंबाधं बध्यतो मानवानां यस्या उद्वतः प्रवतः समं बहु ।
नानावीर्या ओषधीर्या बिभर्ति पृथिवी नः प्रथतां राध्यतां नः ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
asaṃbādhaṃ badhyato mānavānāṃ yasyā udvataḥ pravataḥ samaṃ bahu .
nānāvīryā oṣadhīryā bibharti pṛthivī naḥ prathatāṃ rādhyatāṃ naḥ ..2..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! who extends unimpeded freedom (both outer and inner) to human beings through her mountains, slopes and plains, she bears many plants and medicinal herbs of various potencies; may she extend her riches to us (and make us healthy).
contentBlocks.meanings: असंबाधं (asaṃbādham) – unimpeded; बध्यतः (badhyataḥ) – freedom; मानवानां (mānavānāṃ) – of human beings; यस्या (yasyā) – whose; उद्वतः (udvataḥ) – mountains; प्रवतः (pravataḥ) – slopes; समं (samaṃ) – plains; बहु (bahu) – many; नानावीर्या (nānāvīryā) – of various potencies (nānā:various | vīryā:potencies); ओषधीर्या (oṣadhīryā) – medicinal herbs (oṣadhīḥ:herbs | yā:which); बिभर्ति (bibharti) – bears; पृथिवी (pṛthivī) – earth; नः (naḥ) – to us; प्रथतां (prathatāṃ) – extend; राध्यतां (rādhyatāṃ) – riches; नः (naḥ) – to us;

contentBlocks.original:
यस्यां समुद्र उत सिन्धुरापो यस्यामन्नं कृष्टयः संबभूवुः ।
यस्यामिदं जिन्वति प्राणदेजत्सा नो भूमिः पूर्वपेये दधातु ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāṃ samudra uta sindhurāpo yasyāmannaṃ kṛṣṭayaḥ saṃbabhūvuḥ .
yasyāmidaṃ jinvati prāṇadejatsā no bhūmiḥ pūrvapeye dadhātu ..3..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! in her is woven together ocean and river waters; in her is contained food which she manifests when ploughed, in her indeed is alive all lives; may she bestow us with that life.
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्याम् (yasyām) – in whom; समुद्र (samudra) – ocean; उत (uta) – and; सिन्धुः (sindhuḥ) – river; आपः (āpaḥ) – waters; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in whom; अन्नम् (annam) – food; कृष्टयः (kṛṣṭayaḥ) – ploughed fields; संबभूवुः (saṃbabhūvuḥ) – manifested; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in whom; इदम् (idam) – this; जिन्वति (jinvati) – nourishes; प्राणदेजत् (prāṇadejat) – life-giving (prāṇa:life | dejat:giving); सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – us; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; पूर्वपेये (pūrvapeye) – in the early drink (pūrva:early | peye:drink); दधातु (dadhātu) – may bestow;

contentBlocks.original:
यस्याश्चतस्रः प्रदिशः पृथिव्या यस्यामन्नं कृष्टयः संबभूवुः ।
या बिभर्ति बहुधा प्राणदेजत्सा नो भूमिर्गोष्वप्यन्ने दधातु ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāścatasraḥ pradiśaḥ pṛthivyā yasyāmannaṃ kṛṣṭayaḥ saṃbabhūvuḥ .
yā bibharti bahudhā prāṇadejatsā no bhūmirgoṣvapyanne dadhātu ..4..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! in her resides the four directions of the world; in her is contained food which she manifests when ploughed, she sustains the various lives living in her; may she, the mother earth, bestow on us the ray of life present even in food.
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्याः (yasyāḥ) – of whom; चतस्रः (catasraḥ) – four; प्रदिशः (pradiśaḥ) – directions; पृथिव्या (pṛthivyā) – of the earth; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in whom; अन्नम् (annam) – food; कृष्टयः (kṛṣṭayaḥ) – ploughed fields; संबभूवुः (saṃbabhūvuḥ) – manifested; या (yā) – who; बिभर्ति (bibharti) – sustains; बहुधा (bahudhā) – in many ways; प्राणदेजत् (prāṇadejat) – life-giving; सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – to us; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; गोषु (goṣu) – in cows; अपि (api) – even; अन्ने (anne) – in food; दधातु (dadhātu) – bestow;

contentBlocks.original:
यस्यां पूर्वे पूर्वजना विचक्रिरे यस्यां देवा असुरानभ्यवर्तयन् ।
गवामश्वानां वयसश्च विष्ठा भगं वर्चः पृथिवी नो दधातु ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāṃ pūrve pūrvajanā vicakrire yasyāṃ devā asurānabhyavartayan .
gavāmaśvānāṃ vayasaśca viṣṭhā bhagaṃ varcaḥ pṛthivī no dadhātu ..5..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! in her our forefathers lived and performed (their activities) in earlier times; in her the devas (the good forces) overturned the asuras (the evil forces) (since earlier times), in her lived the cows, horses, birds (and other animals in earlier times); may she, the mother earth, bestow on us prosperity and splendour.
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्याम् (yasyām) – in which; पूर्वे (pūrve) – earlier; पूर्वजनाः (pūrvajanāḥ) – forefathers (pūrva:earlier | janāḥ:people); विचक्रिरे (vicakrire) – performed; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in which; देवाः (devāḥ) – devas; असुरान् (asurān) – asuras; अभ्यवर्तयन् (abhyavartayan) – overturned; गवाम् (gavām) – of cows; अश्वानाम् (aśvānām) – of horses; वयसः (vayasaḥ) – of birds; च (ca) – and; विष्ठा (viṣṭhā) – lived; भगम् (bhagam) – prosperity; वर्चः (varcaḥ) – splendour; पृथिवी (pṛthivī) – earth; नः (naḥ) – us; दधातु (dadhātu) – bestow;

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वंभरा वसुधानी प्रतिष्ठा हिरण्यवक्षा जगतो निवेशनी ।
वैश्वानरं बिभ्रती भूमिरग्निमिन्द्रऋषभा द्रविणे नो दधातु ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvaṃbharā vasudhānī pratiṣṭhā hiraṇyavakṣā jagato niveśanī .
vaiśvānaraṃ bibhratī bhūmiragnimindraṛṣabhā draviṇe no dadhātu ..6..
contentBlocks.translation:
Salutations to mother earth! she is Vishwambhara (all-bearing), she is Vasudhaa (producer of all wealth), she is Pratishtha (foundation on which we live), she is Hiranyavaksha (of golden bosom) and the dwelling place of the world,
she holds the Vaishvanara (the universal fire) within her, the fire which empowers Indra and Rishabha; may the mother earth bestow on us (the splendour of that fire and make us strong).
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वंभरा (viśvaṃbharā) – all-bearing (viśva:all | bharā:bearing); वसुधानी (vasudhānī) – producer of all wealth (vasu:wealth | dhānī:producer); प्रतिष्ठा (pratiṣṭhā) – foundation; हिरण्यवक्षा (hiraṇyavakṣā) – of golden bosom (hiraṇya:golden | vakṣā:bosom); जगतो (jagato) – of the world; निवेशनी (niveśanī) – dwelling place; वैश्वानरं (vaiśvānaraṃ) – universal fire (viśva:universal | anara:fire); बिभ्रती (bibhratī) – holding; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; अग्निम् (agnim) – fire; इन्द्रऋषभा (indraṛṣabhā) – empowers Indra and Rishabha (indra:Indra | ṛṣabhā:Rishabha); द्रविणे (draviṇe) – wealth; नः (naḥ) – us; दधातु (dadhātu) – may bestow;

contentBlocks.original:
यां रक्षन्त्यस्वप्ना विश्वदानीं देवा भूमिं पृथिवीमप्रमादम् ।
सा नो मधु प्रियं दुहामथो उक्षतु वर्चसा ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāṃ rakṣantyasvapnā viśvadānīṃ devā bhūmiṃ pṛthivīmapramādam .
sā no madhu priyaṃ duhāmatho ukṣatu varcasā ..7..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! her, the devas protect sleeplessly with vigilence, she who is the all-giving mother earth, may she milk for us that delightful honey which gives the great splendour (of divinity).
contentBlocks.meanings: याम् (yām) – whom; रक्षन्ति (rakṣanti) – protect; अस्वप्नाः (asvapnāḥ) – sleepless; विश्वदानीम् (viśvadānīm) – all-giving; देवाः (devāḥ) – devas; भूमिम् (bhūmim) – earth; पृथिवीम् (pṛthivīm) – earth; अप्रमादम् (apramādam) – vigilance; सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – for us; मधु (madhu) – honey; प्रियम् (priyam) – delightful; दुहाम् (duhām) – may milk; अथ (atho) – and; उक्षतु (ukṣatu) – may sprinkle; वर्चसा (varcasā) – with splendor;

contentBlocks.original:
यार्णवेऽधि सलिलमग्न आसीद्यां मायाभिरन्वचरन्मनीषिणः ।
यस्या हृदयं परमे व्योमन्त्सत्येनावृतममृतं पृथिव्याः ।
सा नो भूमिस्त्विषिं बलं राष्ट्रे दधातूत्तमे ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yārṇave'dhi salilamagna āsīdyāṃ māyābhiranvacaranmanīṣiṇaḥ .
yasyā hṛdayaṃ parame vyomantsatyenāvṛtamamṛtaṃ pṛthivyāḥ .
sā no bhūmistviṣiṃ balaṃ rāṣṭre dadhātūttame ..8..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! sitting above sea as well as lying immersed in its waters (in meditation), the sages pursued her by supernatural powers (i.e. tried to understand her real nature by yogic powers), (they found that) the heart of mother earth lies in the highest vyoman (spiritual sky) enveloped by truth and immortality, may she, the mother earth, bestow her splendorous vigour on us and our great kingdom.
contentBlocks.meanings: यार्णवे (yārṇave) – above the sea (yā:who | arṇave:in the sea); अधि (adhi) – above; सलिलमग्न (salilamagna) – immersed in water (salila:water | magna:immersed); आसीत् (āsīt) – was; यां (yām) – whom; मायाभिः (māyābhiḥ) – by supernatural powers (māyā:supernatural power | bhiḥ:by); अन्वचरन् (anvacaran) – pursued; मनीषिणः (manīṣiṇaḥ) – sages; यस्याः (yasyāḥ) – whose; हृदयं (hṛdayam) – heart; परमे (parame) – in the highest; व्योमन् (vyoman) – sky; सत्येन (satyena) – by truth; आवृतम् (āvṛtam) – enveloped; अमृतम् (amṛtam) – immortality; पृथिव्याः (pṛthivyāḥ) – of the earth; सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – our; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; त्विषिम् (tviṣim) – splendorous vigour; बलम् (balam) – strength; राष्ट्रे (rāṣṭre) – in the kingdom; दधातु (dadhātu) – may bestow; उत्तमे (uttame) – great;

contentBlocks.original:
यस्यामापः परिचराः समानीरहोरात्रे अप्रमादं क्षरन्ति ।
सा नो भूमिर्भूरिधारा पयो दुहामथो उक्षतु वर्चसा ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāmāpaḥ paricarāḥ samānīrahorātre apramādaṃ kṣaranti .
sā no bhūmirbhūridhārā payo duhāmatho ukṣatu varcasā ..9..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! in her the waters flow on all sides day and night with vigilence (i.e. unceasingly), may she, the mother earth give us the milk of her abundant streams, and moisten us with its splendour (present in water).
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्याम् (yasyām) – in which; आपः (āpaḥ) – waters; परिचराः (paricarāḥ) – attendants; समानी (samānī) – together; अहोरात्रे (ahorātre) – day and night (aho:day | rātre:night); अप्रमादं (apramādam) – unceasingly; क्षरन्ति (kṣaranti) – flow; सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – us; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; भूरिधारा (bhūridhārā) – abundant streams (bhūri:abundant | dhārā:streams); पयः (payaḥ) – milk; दुहाम् (duhām) – give; अथो (atho) – and; उक्षतु (ukṣatu) – moisten; वर्चसा (varcasā) – with splendor;

contentBlocks.original:
यामश्विनावमिमातां विष्णुर्यस्यां विचक्रमे ।
इन्द्रो यां चक्र आत्मनेऽनमित्रां शचीपतिः ।
सा नो भूमिर्वि सृजतां माता पुत्राय मे पयः ॥१०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāmaśvināvamimātāṃ viṣṇuryasyāṃ vicakrame .
indro yāṃ cakra ātmane'namitrāṃ śacīpatiḥ .
sā no bhūmirvi sṛjatāṃ mātā putrāya me payaḥ ..10..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! her, the aswins (divine physicians) have measured out (i.e. filled her with herbs and healing qualities), in her Vishnu strode (imparting her with divine qualities), Indra, the husband of Shachi, made her soul free from enemies (i.e. made her soul the friend of all like a mother to a son), may she pour forth her milk (with kindness) as a mother does to her son.
contentBlocks.meanings: याम् (yām) – whom; अश्विनौ (aśvinau) – the Ashvins; अमिमाताम् (amimātām) – measured out; विष्णुः (viṣṇuḥ) – Vishnu; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in whom; विचक्रमे (vicakrame) – strode; इन्द्रः (indraḥ) – Indra; याम् (yām) – whom; चक्र (cakra) – made; आत्मने (ātmane) – for himself; अनमित्राम् (anamitrām) – free from enemies; शचीपतिः (śacīpatiḥ) – husband of Shachi (śacī:Shachi | patiḥ:husband); सा (sā) – she; नः (naḥ) – for us; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; वि सृजताम् (vi sṛjatām) – may pour forth; माता (mātā) – mother; पुत्राय (putrāya) – to the son; मे (me) – my; पयः (payaḥ) – milk;

contentBlocks.original:
गिरयस्ते पर्वता हिमवन्तोऽरण्यं ते पृथिवि स्योनमस्तु ।
बभ्रुं कृष्णां रोहिणीं विश्वरूपां ध्रुवां भूमिं पृथिवीमिन्द्रगुप्ताम् ।
अजीतेऽहतो अक्षतोऽध्यष्ठां पृथिवीमहम् ॥११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
girayaste parvatā himavanto'raṇyaṃ te pṛthivi syonamastu .
babhruṃ kṛṣṇāṃ rohiṇīṃ viśvarūpāṃ dhruvāṃ bhūmiṃ pṛthivīmindraguptām .
ajīte'hato akṣato'dhyaṣṭhāṃ pṛthivīmaham ..11..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! O mother earth, may your hills and snow-clad mountains (spread its coolness within us); may your forests spread its delight within us, you present a vishwarupa with your many colours - Babhru (brown) (of mountains), Krishna (blue) (of rivers), Rohini (red) (of flowers); (but behind all these enchanting appearances) O mother earth, you are like Dhruva - firm and immovable; and you are protected by Indra, (on your firm foundation) which is unconquered, unslayed and unbroken whole, I stand firm (and whole, O mother).
contentBlocks.meanings: गिरयः (girayaḥ) – mountains; ते (te) – your; पर्वताः (parvatāḥ) – hills; हिमवन्तः (himavantaḥ) – snow-clad; अरण्यम् (araṇyam) – forest; ते (te) – your; पृथिवि (pṛthivi) – earth; स्योनम् (syonam) – pleasant; अस्तु (astu) – may be; बभ्रुम् (babhrum) – brown; कृष्णाम् (kṛṣṇām) – black; रोहिणीम् (rohiṇīm) – red; विश्वरूपाम् (viśvarūpām) – universal form (viśva:universal | rūpām:form); ध्रुवाम् (dhruvām) – firm; भूमिम् (bhūmim) – earth; पृथिवीम् (pṛthivīm) – earth; इन्द्रगुप्ताम् (indraguptām) – protected by Indra (indra:Indra | guptām:protected); अजीते (ajīte) – unconquered; अहतः (ahataḥ) – unslayed; अक्षतः (akṣataḥ) – unbroken; अध्यष्ठाम् (adhyaṣṭhām) – I stand; पृथिवीम् (pṛthivīm) – earth; अहम् (aham) – I;

contentBlocks.original:
यत्ते मध्यं पृथिवि यच्च नभ्यं यास्त ऊर्जस्तन्वः संबभूवुः ।
तासु नो धेह्यभि नः पवस्व माता भूमिः पुत्रो अहं पृथिव्याः ।
पर्जन्यः पिता स उ नः पिपर्तु ॥१२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yatte madhyaṃ pṛthivi yacca nabhyaṃ yāsta ūrjastanvaḥ saṃbabhūvuḥ .
tāsu no dhehyabhi naḥ pavasva mātā bhūmiḥ putro ahaṃ pṛthivyāḥ .
parjanyaḥ pitā sa u naḥ pipartu ..12..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! in your center, O mother earth, is your navel from which the vital power emanates and spreads out, absorb us in that power and purify us, O bhoomi mata, i am the son of mother earth, parjanya (rain god) is my father, may he fill us (with the vital power in water).
contentBlocks.meanings: यत् (yat) – which; ते (te) – your; मध्यं (madhyaṃ) – center; पृथिवि (pṛthivi) – earth; यत् (yat) – which; च (ca) – and; नभ्यम् (nabhyaṃ) – navel; याः (yāḥ) – which; ते (te) – your; ऊर्जः (ūrjaḥ) – vital power; तन्वः (tanvaḥ) – bodies; संबभूवुः (saṃbabhūvuḥ) – emanated; तासु (tāsu) – in them; नः (naḥ) – us; धेहि (dhehi) – place; अभि (abhi) – towards; नः (naḥ) – us; पवस्व (pavasva) – purify; माता (mātā) – mother; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; पुत्रः (putraḥ) – son; अहम् (aham) – I; पृथिव्याः (pṛthivyāḥ) – of the earth; पर्जन्यः (parjanyaḥ) – rain god; पिता (pitā) – father; सः (saḥ) – he; उ (u) – indeed; नः (naḥ) – us; पिपर्तु (pipartu) – may fill;

contentBlocks.original:
यस्यां वेदिं परिगृह्णन्ति भूम्यां यस्यां यज्ञं तन्वते विश्वकर्माणः ।
यस्यां मीयन्ते स्वरवः पृथिव्यामूर्ध्वाः शुक्रा आहुत्याः पुरस्तात् ।
सा नो भूमिर्वर्धयद्वर्धमाना ॥१३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāṃ vediṃ parigṛhṇanti bhūmyāṃ yasyāṃ yajñaṃ tanvate viśvakarmāṇaḥ .
yasyāṃ mīyante svaravaḥ pṛthivyāmūrdhvāḥ śukrā āhutyāḥ purastāt .
sā no bhūmirvardhayadvardhamānā ..13..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! in her, bhoomi (ground) has spread herself as the sacrificial altar; in her, all the activities of the world has spread themselves as yagya, in her, from the beginning, the sounds (of activities) of the world (resembling the chants of yagya) during oblations rises up and disappears in the purifying upper layers (symbolically purifying the workers), may the expansion (expanding space) provided by the earth, expand (our inner selves also).
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्याम् (yasyām) – in which; वेदिम् (vedim) – altar; परिगृह्णन्ति (parigṛhṇanti) – they take; भूम्याम् (bhūmyām) – on the earth; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in which; यज्ञम् (yajñam) – sacrifice; तन्वते (tanvate) – they perform; विश्वकर्माणः (viśvakarmāṇaḥ) – all workers; यस्याम् (yasyām) – in which; मीयन्ते (mīyante) – are measured; स्वरवः (svaravaḥ) – sounds; पृथिव्याम् (pṛthivyām) – on the earth; ऊर्ध्वाः (ūrdhvāḥ) – upwards; शुक्राः (śukrāḥ) – pure; आहुत्याः (āhutyāḥ) – oblations; पुरस्तात् (purastāt) – in the beginning; सा (sā) – that; नः (naḥ) – our; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; वर्धयत् (vardhayat) – may expand; वर्धमाना (vardhamānā) – expanding;

contentBlocks.original:
यो नो द्वेषत्पृथिवी यः पृतन्याद्योऽभिदासान्मनसा यो वधेन ।
तं नो भूमे रन्धय पूर्वकृत्वरि ॥१४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yo no dveṣatpṛthivī yaḥ pṛtanyādyo'bhidāsānmanasā yo vadhena .
taṃ no bhūme randhaya pūrvakṛtvari ..14..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! he who hates us, O earth, he who attacks us or mentally considers us as enemies, or he who strikes us, him, O mother earth, subdue, as you have done since earliest times.
contentBlocks.meanings: यः (yaḥ) – who; नः (naḥ) – us; द्वेषत् (dveṣat) – hates; पृथिवी (pṛthivī) – earth; यः (yaḥ) – who; पृतन्यात् (pṛtanyāt) – attacks; यः (yaḥ) – who; अभिदासान् (abhidāsān) – considers as enemies; मनसा (manasā) – mentally; यः (yaḥ) – who; वधेन (vadhena) – with striking; तम् (tam) – him; नः (naḥ) – us; भूमे (bhūme) – O earth; रन्धय (randhaya) – subdue; पूर्वकृत्वरि (pūrvakṛtvari) – as done since earliest times (pūrva:earliest | kṛt:done | vari:times);

contentBlocks.original:
त्वज्जातास्त्वयि चरन्ति मर्त्यास्त्वं बिभर्षि द्विपदस्त्वं चतुष्पदः ।
तवेमे पृथिवि पञ्च मानवा येभ्यो ज्योतिरमृतं मर्त्येभ्य उद्यन्त्सूर्यो रश्मिभिरातनोति ॥१५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvajjātāstvayi caranti martyāstvaṃ bibharṣi dvipadastvaṃ catuṣpadaḥ .
taveme pṛthivi pañca mānavā yebhyo jyotiramṛtaṃ martyebhya udyantsūryo raśmibhirātanoti ..15..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! (first those) produced by you, (second) those moving about in you, (third) those two-footed and (fourth) those four-footed ones; (all of) whom you bear in the land of mortality, fifth is the manava, from whom the light of immortality emanates (even) from the land of mortality, which O mother earth, you diffuse with the rays of the rising sun (i.e. the essence of immortality pervades in you).
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वज्जाताः (tvajjātāḥ) – produced by you (tvat:by you | jātāḥ:produced); त्वयि (tvayi) – in you; चरन्ति (caranti) – move about; मर्त्याः (martyāḥ) – mortals; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; बिभर्षि (bibharṣi) – bear; द्विपदः (dvipadaḥ) – two-footed; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; चतुष्पदः (catuṣpadaḥ) – four-footed; तव (tava) – your; इमे (ime) – these; पृथिवि (pṛthivi) – earth; पञ्च (pañca) – five; मानवाः (mānavāḥ) – humans; येभ्यः (yebhyaḥ) – from whom; ज्योतिरमृतं (jyotiramṛtaṃ) – light of immortality (jyotis:light | amṛtaṃ:immortality); मर्त्येभ्यः (martyebhyaḥ) – from mortals; उद्यन् (udyant) – rising; सूर्यः (sūryaḥ) – sun; रश्मिभिः (raśmibhiḥ) – with rays; आतनुति (ātanoti) – diffuses;

contentBlocks.original: ता नः प्रजाः सं दुह्रतां समग्रा वाचो मधु पृथिवी धेहि मह्यम् ॥१६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: tā naḥ prajāḥ saṃ duhratāṃ samagrā vāco madhu pṛthivī dhehi mahyam ..16..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! (the essence of immortality pervades in you) may we, your children (be able to) together milk the ritam (divine order) present everywhere in you, O mother earth, by absorbing (i.e. understanding) the great honeyed speech (vedic mantras).
contentBlocks.meanings: ता (tā) – those; नः (naḥ) – our; प्रजाः (prajāḥ) – offspring; सं (saṃ) – together; दुह्रतां (duhratāṃ) – may milk; समग्रा (samagrā) – complete; वाचः (vācaḥ) – speech; मधु (madhu) – honeyed; पृथिवी (pṛthivī) – earth; धेहि (dhehi) – place; मह्यम् (mahyam) – for me;

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वस्वं मातरमोषधीनां ध्रुवां भूमिं पृथिवीं धर्मणा धृताम् ।
शिवां स्योनामनु चरेम विश्वहा ॥१७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvasvaṃ mātaramoṣadhīnāṃ dhruvāṃ bhūmiṃ pṛthivīṃ dharmaṇā dhṛtām .
śivāṃ syonāmanu carema viśvahā ..17..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! the herbs (plants) which are like mothers of the world (who sustains us) grows on the immovable earth (bhoomi); the earth which is held by dharma, and in which auspiciousness gently pervades throughout the world.
contentBlocks.meanings: विश्वस्वं (viśvasvam) – of the universe (viśva:universe | svam:own); मातरम् (mātaram) – mother; ओषधीनां (oṣadhīnām) – of the herbs; ध्रुवां (dhruvām) – immovable; भूमिं (bhūmiṃ) – earth; पृथिवीं (pṛthivīṃ) – earth; धर्मणा (dharmaṇā) – by dharma; धृताम् (dhṛtām) – held; शिवां (śivām) – auspicious; स्योनाम् (syonām) – gentle; अनु (anu) – throughout; चरेम (carema) – we may move; विश्वहा (viśvahā) – pervades the world (viśva:world | hā:pervades);

contentBlocks.original:
महत्सधस्थं महती बभूविथ महान्वेग एजथुर्वेपथुष्टे ।
महांस्त्वेन्द्रो रक्षत्यप्रमादम् ।
सा नो भूमे प्र रोचय हिरण्यस्येव संदृशि मा नो द्विक्षत कश्चन ॥१८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahatsadhasthaṃ mahatī babhūvitha mahānvega ejathurvepathuṣṭe .
mahāṃstvendro rakṣatyapramādam .
sā no bhūme pra rocaya hiraṇyasyeva saṃdṛśi mā no dvikṣata kaścana ..18..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to mother earth! great is this place where we stand together (i.e. live together); mighty is the force present in it, which controls its great speed of movement and shaking, great is the god indra who protects her with vigilence (day and night), (in this great meeting place enveloped by divine powers) may she the bhoomi (earth), make us lustrous like gold so that we do not see anyone with the attitude of hatred.
contentBlocks.meanings: महत्सधस्थं (mahatsadhastham) – in the great assembly (mahat:great | sadhastham:in the assembly); महती (mahatī) – great; बभूविथ (babhūvitha) – became; महान्वेग (mahānvega) – great speed (mahān:great | vega:speed); एजथुर्वेपथुष्टे (ejathurvepathuṣṭe) – shaking with great speed (ejathu:shaking | vepathuṣṭe:with great speed); महांस्त्वेन्द्रो (mahāṃstvandro) – great Indra (mahān:great | indraḥ:Indra); रक्षत्यप्रमादम् (rakṣatyapramādam) – protects vigilantly (rakṣati:protects | apramādam:vigilantly); सा (sā) – she; नो (no) – us; भूमे (bhūme) – earth; प्र (pra) – forth; रोचय (rocaya) – make lustrous; हिरण्यस्येव (hiraṇyasyeva) – like gold (hiraṇyasya:of gold | iva:like); संदृशि (saṃdṛśi) – in appearance; मा (mā) – not; नो (no) – us; द्विक्षत (dvikṣata) – see with hatred; कश्चन (kaścana) – anyone;
